title: 1 Gallon Equals 4 Quarts
description: Recall the customary unit equivalence between gallons and quarts.
condition: Asked for the relationship between gallons and quarts.
response: State that 1 gallon equals 4 quarts.
label: Fact
notes: Memorized customary conversion.

title: 1 Minute Equals 60 Seconds
description: Recall the time conversion between minutes and seconds.
condition: Asked for the relationship between minutes and seconds.
response: State that 1 minute equals 60 seconds.
label: Fact
notes: Memorized time conversion.

title: 1 Pint Equals 2 Cups
description: Recall the customary unit equivalence between pints and cups.
condition: Asked for the relationship between pints and cups.
response: State that 1 pint equals 2 cups.
label: Fact
notes: Memorized customary conversion.

title: 1 Pound Equals 16 Ounces
description: Recall the customary weight conversion between pounds and ounces.
condition: Asked how many ounces are in 1 pound.
response: State that 1 pound equals 16 ounces.
label: Fact
notes: Required to select 16 as the correct multiple.

title: Add One Group for Nonzero Remainder in Context
description: When a division with remainder represents grouping objects into containers, add one more group if the remainder is not zero.
condition: A division a ÷ b results in quotient q and remainder r > 0, and the context requires holding all items in groups of size b.
response: Compute total groups as q + 1.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Applies only when remainder represents leftover items needing an additional full container.

title: Add Partial Products
description: Add the partial products from each place value to find the total product.
condition: Given multiple partial products from multiplying each place value part of a number.
response: Add the partial products to compute the final product.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Relies on place value understanding and addition. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Add Partial Quotients
description: Combine partial quotients to determine the total quotient.
condition: Given several partial quotients from division steps.
response: Add the partial quotients to obtain the final quotient.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Often implicit in area model and partial quotients strategies.

title: Apply Associative Property of Multiplication
description: Regroup factors in a multiplication expression without changing the product.
condition: A multiplication expression with three factors, such as (a × b) × c or a × (b × c).
response: Regroup the factors to an equivalent form to simplify computation.
label: Principle / Model

title: Apply Distributive Property
description: Use the distributive property to multiply a number by a sum by multiplying each addend separately and adding the results.
condition: Given a product where one factor is decomposed into a sum (a(b + c)).
response: Compute a×b and a×c, then add the products.
label: Principle
notes: Explicitly assessed in expression selection. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Apply Long Division with Place Value
description: Carry out standard long division by dividing, multiplying, subtracting, and bringing down digits in place-value order.
condition: Given a multi-digit dividend and one-digit divisor in long-division format.
response: Divide the highest place value, record the quotient digit in that place, multiply, subtract, bring down the next digit, and repeat.
label: Principle / Model

title: Apply Place Value in Multiplication
description: Recognize that multiplying by tens or hundreds scales the product by powers of ten.
condition: Given a factor that is a multiple of 10 or 100 (e.g., 300).
response: Multiply the non-zero digits and append the appropriate number of zeros.
label: Principle / Model

title: Check Division with Multiplication
description: Verify a quotient by multiplying it by the divisor to see if it equals the dividend.
condition: Given a division equation and a proposed quotient.
response: Multiply divisor × quotient and compare the product to the dividend.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Area Model Cell Product
description: Multiply the row and column values in an area model cell.
condition: Given one row value and one column value in an area model (e.g., 6 and 90).
response: Multiply them to fill in the cell (e.g., 6 × 90 = 540).
label: Production / Skill
notes: Repeated for each region of the area model.

title: Compute Total Cost for Multiple Identical Groups
description: Find total cost by multiplying the per-group total by the number of groups.
condition: Each of n groups receives the same set of items with known total cost per group.
response: Multiply cost per group by n.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Whole-Number Division with Remainder
description: Use standard division to find quotient and remainder for multi-digit dividends.
condition: Given whole numbers a and b where a is not a multiple of b.
response: Compute quotient q and remainder r such that a = bq + r and 0 ≤ r < b.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Algorithmic long division process.

title: Convert Gallons and Quarts to Total Quarts
description: Convert a mixed measurement in gallons and quarts into total quarts.
condition: A quantity given in gallons and quarts that must be expressed only in quarts.
response: Multiply gallons by 4, then add the remaining quarts.
label: Principle / Model
example: Convert $3$ gallons $1$ quart to quarts: $3 \times 4 = 12$ quarts; $12 + 1 = 13$ quarts.
notes: Builds on knowing 1 gallon = 4 quarts.

title: Convert Minutes to Seconds
description: Convert a given number of minutes into seconds.
condition: A time measurement in minutes that must be expressed in seconds.
response: Multiply the number of minutes by 60.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires recalling that 1 minute = 60 seconds.

title: Convert Pints to Cups
description: Convert a given number of pints into cups.
condition: A volume given in pints that must be expressed in cups.
response: Multiply the number of pints by 2.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires recalling that 1 pint = 2 cups.

title: Convert Quarts to Pints
description: Use the fixed conversion that 1 quart equals 2 pints.
condition: Given a quantity in quarts.
response: Multiply the number of quarts by 2 to find pints.
label: Concept
notes: Conversion factor is conventional knowledge.

title: Decompose Factors for Area Model
description: Rewrite factors into tens and ones to construct an area model.
condition: Given a two-digit multiplication problem (e.g., 26 × 97).
response: Decompose each factor into tens and ones (e.g., 26 = 20 + 6; 97 = 90 + 7).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Supports use of distributive property in an area model.

title: Decompose Number by Place Value
description: Break a multi-digit number into hundreds, tens, and ones (or thousands, hundreds, tens, ones).
condition: Given a multi-digit number in standard form.
response: Rewrite it as a sum of its place value components.
label: Principle
notes: Foundational for area models and distributive property. [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Decompose Number into Factor and Power of Ten
description: Rewrite a multiple of 10, 100, or 1,000 as a single-digit factor times a power of ten.
condition: A number such as 700, 5,000, or 2,000 that is a multiple of 10, 100, or 1,000.
response: Express the number as (single-digit or basic factor) × (power of ten).
label: Principle / Model

title: Divide by Single-Digit Divisor
description: Compute the quotient when dividing a whole number by a one-digit divisor.
condition: Given an expression of the form a ÷ b where b is a one-digit number.
response: Compute the whole-number quotient (with remainder if applicable).
label: Production / Skill
notes: Procedural fluency with basic division facts and multi-digit computation.

title: Estimate Division Using Compatible Numbers
description: Estimate a quotient by rounding numbers to compatible values for easier division.
condition: Given a division situation requiring an estimate (often in word problems).
response: Round dividend and/or divisor to nearby compatible numbers and divide to get an approximate quotient.
label: Principle
notes: Used to justify reasonableness.

title: Evaluate Correctness of Partial Products
description: Determine whether each partial product in a student's work matches correct place-value multiplication.
condition: Given a completed partial products solution for a two-digit by two-digit multiplication problem.
response: Check each partial product and identify whether the overall work is correct.
label: Concept
notes: Leads to selecting the correctly completed solution (Carla).

title: Evaluate Expression and Match to Given Value
description: Compute an expression and select the matching value from a list of answer choices.
condition: An arithmetic expression and a fixed list of possible numeric answers.
response: Evaluate the expression and choose the equal value from the list.
label: Production / Skill

title: Identify Place Value Error
description: Recognize when a multiplication or division step ignores place value (e.g., treating 90 as 9).
condition: Given worked steps where a partial product or quotient digit does not match correct place value multiplication/division.
response: State that the error comes from incorrect place value (e.g., 8×90 computed as 72 instead of 720).
label: Principle
example: Noting that $8\times90=720$, not 72, because 90 has a tens value.
notes: Supports error analysis explanations.

title: Identify Quotient as Size of Each Group
description: Recognize that in equal-group division, the quotient represents the size of each group.
condition: A division situation describes total items split into a given number of equal groups.
response: State that the quotient is the size of each group.
label: Concept
example: In 78 ÷ 3 = 26, 26 is the size of each group.
notes: Specific interpretation tied to partitive division context.

title: Interpret Area Model for Division
description: Interpret an area/array model by recognizing that the total area is decomposed into partial products equal to divisor × partial quotients.
condition: Given an area model representing division with one side labeled as the divisor and interior regions labeled as partial products.
response: Identify that each region equals divisor × partial quotient and that the sum of partial quotients is the total quotient.
label: Principle
example: If 9 is the divisor and the regions are 9,000, 630, and 27, then the partial quotients are 1,000, 70, and 3 because $9\times1{,}000=9{,}000$, $9\times70=630$, $9\times3=27$.
notes: Connects area model to division as inverse of multiplication. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Interpret Area Model for Multiplication
description: Recognize that each cell in an area model represents the product of a factor and a place value part.
condition: Given an area model with one factor on one side and place value parts of the other factor on the top.
response: Identify each cell as the product of the corresponding row and column values.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the model shows $7$ on the side and $900, 30, 8$ on top, the cells are $7 \times 900 = 6,300$, $7 \times 30 = 210$, $7 \times 8 = 56$.
notes: Connects visual model to distributive property.

title: Interpret Place Value Units (Ones, Tens, Hundreds)
description: Translate between place value units and standard form numbers.
condition: A quantity expressed in units such as '2 tens' or '6 hundreds.'
response: Convert to standard number form by multiplying the unit value.
label: Principle / Model

title: Match Expression to Product
description: Select the correct numerical product that corresponds to a multiplication expression.
condition: Given a multiplication expression and a list of possible products.
response: Choose the product equal to the computed result.
label: Category
notes: Requires correct computation before matching.

title: Multiply by a Power of Ten
description: Multiply a number by 10, 100, or 1,000 by shifting place value.
condition: A multiplication expression where one factor is 10, 100, or 1,000.
response: Append the appropriate number of zeros or shift digits left accordingly.
label: Principle / Model

title: Multiply for Unit Conversion
description: Convert between units when relationship is multiplicative (e.g., 1 gallon = 4 quarts).
condition: Given a quantity in larger units and a constant conversion rate.
response: Multiply by the conversion factor to find the equivalent quantity in smaller units.
label: Principle
notes: Constant conversion factor of 4 quarts per gallon.

title: Multiply Single Digit by Place Value Unit
description: Multiply a single-digit number by a number representing a specific place value (hundreds, tens, ones).
condition: Expression of the form $a \times (b \text{ with place value, e.g., } 400, 30, 8)$.
response: Compute the product while preserving correct place value.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires understanding of basic facts and place value scaling.

title: Multiply Single-Digit by Multi-Digit Number
description: Compute the product of a single-digit number and a multi-digit number using place value or standard algorithm.
condition: Expression of the form $a \times b$ where $a$ is a single digit and $b$ is a multi-digit number.
response: Calculate the total product.
label: Principle / Model
notes: May be solved via distributive property or standard algorithm.

title: Regroup When Dividing Across Place Values
description: Regroup a larger place value into smaller units when it cannot be evenly divided.
condition: When a place value (e.g., hundreds) cannot be evenly divided by the divisor.
response: Convert the remaining value into the next smaller place value (e.g., 1 hundred = 10 tens) and continue dividing.
label: Rule / Plan
example: In $456 ÷ 3$, after dividing 4 hundreds to get 1 hundred with 1 hundred left, regroup 1 hundred as 10 tens to make 15 tens.
notes: Key conceptual step in place value chart division.

title: Represent Division Using a = bq + r
description: Express a division with remainder in the form dividend = divisor × quotient + remainder.
condition: Given quotient q and remainder r from dividing a by b.
response: Write a = bq + r.
label: Principle / Model

title: Select Correct Area Model
description: Select the area model that correctly represents a given division expression using place value partial quotients.
condition: Given a division expression (e.g., 9,657 ÷ 9) and multiple area models with labeled partial quotients.
response: Choose the model where divisor × each partial quotient equals each decomposed part of the dividend and partial quotients sum to the correct quotient.
label: Concept
example: For $9{,}657\div9$, select the model with 1,000, 70, and 3 since $9\times(1{,}000+70+3)=9{,}657$.
notes: Requires checking place value accuracy.

title: Standard Division Algorithm
description: Use the long division algorithm to divide multi-digit numbers by one-digit divisors.
condition: Given a multi-digit dividend and one-digit divisor.
response: Divide stepwise by place value, multiply, subtract, bring down, and repeat to find the quotient.
label: Principle
example: $147\div3=49$ by dividing 14 tens → 4 tens, subtracting 12 tens, bringing down 7 ones, then 9 ones.
notes: Includes correct place value placement in quotient.

title: Subtract to Find Remaining Amount
description: Subtract a known part from a total to find the remaining quantity.
condition: Given a total amount and a portion removed or sold.
response: Compute total minus part to determine remainder.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Used before dividing into groups.

title: Use Partial Quotients in Long Division
description: Subtract large multiples of the divisor from the dividend to build the quotient step by step.
condition: Given a multi-digit dividend and one-digit divisor using partial quotients or long division format.
response: Subtract successive multiples of the divisor and add the partial quotients to obtain the final quotient.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Emphasizes reasoning with large place-value multiples.
